tluaFunctionType.tlua(31,7): error TLUA2322: Type '{ n: number; }' is not assignable to type 'function'.
  Type '{ n: number; }' provides no match for the signature '(...: any): (any?, ...any)'.
tluaFunctionType.tlua(32,14): error TLUA2304: Cannot find name 'LuaFunction'.
tluaFunctionType.tlua(53,16): error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
tluaFunctionType.tlua(57,6): error TLUA2457: Type alias name cannot be 'function'.
tluaFunctionType.tlua(57,15): error TLUA1003: Identifier expected.
tluaFunctionType.tlua(57,17): error TLUA2693: 'number' only refers to a type, but is being used as a value here.
tluaFunctionType.tlua(58,1): error TLUA1005: 'end' expected.


==== tluaFunctionType.tlua (7 errors) ====
    // `function` is the keyword type for the runtime tag `type(x) == "function"`:
    // the top of every Lua function -- variadic parameters, any results. It replaced
    // the LuaFunction lib alias, which no longer exists.
    
    local f: function = function(): number return 1; end;
    declare withParams: (a: number, b: string) => boolean;
    local f2: function = withParams;
    
    // Everywhere a type goes: unions, type arguments, tuples, returns, parameters.
    declare maybeFn: function | nil;
    declare fnTable: Table<string, function>;
    declare fnPair: [function, number];
    declare function pick(chooser: function): function;
    local picked: function = pick(f);
    
    // Calling a `function`-typed value yields the any results it promises, and the
    // rest parameter admits any argument list.
    local first, second = f();
    local one: number | nil = first;
    local viaArgs = f(1, "x", true);
    local a2, b2 = f2(nil, { n = 1 });
    
    // A declared non-callable can never be "function": the guard narrows to never.
    declare plainT: { n: number };
    if type(plainT) == "function" then
        local imp: never = plainT;
    end
    
    // A table is not a function, and the old alias name is gone.
    declare tbl: { n: number };
    local notFn: function = tbl;
          ~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type '{ n: number; }' is not assignable to type 'function'.
!!! error TLUA2322:   Type '{ n: number; }' provides no match for the signature '(...: any): (any?, ...any)'.
    declare old: LuaFunction;
                 ~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2304: Cannot find name 'LuaFunction'.
    
    // `type(x) == "function"` narrows to the keyword type and displays as such.
    declare u: unknown;
    if type(u) == "function" then
        local uf: function = u;
    end
    
    // Narrowing makes a bare type parameter callable (T & function)...
    function apply<T>(v: T): number
        if type(v) == "function" then
            return v(1);
        end
        return 0;
    end
    
    // ...while declared signatures keep their precision instead of widening to the
    // all-any top type.
    declare fnOrString: ((a: number) => void) | string;
    if type(fnOrString) == "function" then
        fnOrString(1);
        fnOrString("x");
                   ~~~
!!! error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
    end
    
    // The spelling is a hard keyword: it cannot name a type alias.
    type function = number;
         ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2457: Type alias name cannot be 'function'.
                  ~
!!! error TLUA1003: Identifier expected.
                    ~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2693: 'number' only refers to a type, but is being used as a value here.
    
    
!!! error TLUA1005: 'end' expected.
